description:
Консерванты продлевают срок хранения продуктов и защищают их от порчи микроорганизмов: бактерий, плесневых грибов и
дрожжей, среди которых могут быть патогенные и непатогенные виды. С этими ингредиентами удается предотвратить развитие
вредоносной микрофлоры или замедлить в ней обмен веществ.
Выбор консервантов и их дозировок зависит от степени бактериальной загрязненности, условий хранения и желаемого срока
годности готового продукта.

preservatives_antioxidants_emulsifying_s.description: Preservatives prolong product shelf life and protect them from spoilage by microorganisms: bacteria, molds, and yeasts, including pathogenic and non-pathogenic species. With these ingredients, it is possible to prevent the development of harmful microflora or slow down their metabolism. The choice of preservatives and their dosages depends on the degree of bacterial contamination, storage conditions, and the desired shelf life of the finished product. **Advantages:** Many of these products are of natural origin, do not contain 'E' index additives, protect products from undesirable microflora (yeast, mold, listeria), increase the shelf life of dairy products, and are thermostable. They can also effectively suppress the growth of yeast and mold without inhibiting lactic acid bacteria, and some enhance their antimicrobial activity in the presence of salts and acids. They generally do not alter the organoleptic properties of the product, are non-toxic, and do not exhibit carcinogenic properties. Some also serve as acidity regulators, emulsifiers, complexing agents, and melting salts. **Application areas:** Sour cream products, yogurt, fermented milk products, curd products, condensed milk, cheeses, processed cheeses, spreads, and milk.
